rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS DONATIELLO VS. ANDRE NEUMANN-LORECK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Confirming Arbitration Awards
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 4, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS DONATIELLO'S Notice Of Motion For Order Confirming Arbitration Awards. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order and a proposed judgment without any unfilled spaces and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TALITHA BALL et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 4, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFFS TALITHA BALL, TELITHA BALL AND DESSIA PATTERSON'S Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration. Denied. Plaintiff's motion is procedurally deficient because Plaintiff fails to present a satisfactory explanation as to why the newly discovered evidence could not have been produced earlier or why the newly discovered evidence was not raised at the hearing or by way of a supplemental brief. Moreover, even if the newly discovered evidence is considered, Plaintiff has failed to present any legal basis why the Court should reconsider its Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER RYS VS. JOSEPH BRADFORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 4, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER RYS' Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. Granted and the trial is continued to December 3, 2012 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery and summary motion dates triggered off the new trial date. The strong policy of liberally granting amendments and any prejudice to defendants eliminated by the short trial continuance mandates the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MEREDITH I RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Wednesday, April 4, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT MASON MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC. FKA PRUDENTIAL Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories - Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions awarded against plaintiff Martin Eng in the amount of $1,074 (assuming no hearing). The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and: (1) the Motion is not opposed. (2) Plaintiff Martin Eng has not submitted any response or proposed response to the Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request for Production, or Requests for Admission. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: 3514 SOUTH, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. 140 SOUTH HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, INC., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 4, 2012, Line 6. PLAINTIFF 3514 SOUTH, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY CORPORATION'S Motion For Attorney Fees. Granted in large part and denied in small part. Attorney's fees are recoverable per Corporations Code 7237(d) and Section 4.6 of 140 South Homeowner's Association Bylaws. The amount of fees and costs sought for defending the cross-complaint is reasonable. The $10,000 in fees sought for this motion is excessive given the relatively modest amount of work needed for the motion. $3000 is awarded for fees for this motion. Accordingly, the total amount awarded is $86,418.04. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/HEK/SM)
